main_dirt.bias_notes: A standard 1-mile dirt oval with a generous 995-foot home stretch. While generally fair, it can occasionally develop a strong inside/speed bias depending on weather and track maintenance. The long stretch gives 'Swoopers' (closers) ample runway to catch tiring front-runners in two-turn route races.
main_dirt.post_bias: In 5.5f and 6f sprints, inside and middle posts are preferred, particularly for horses with early speed. In two-turn routes, inside posts (1-4) hold a tactical advantage for saving ground around the turns.

main_turf.bias_notes: Set inside the main track, this is a much tighter 7/8-mile (7 furlongs) oval. Because of the sharper turns and relatively short straightaway, tactical early speed and rail-skimming trips are absolutely critical. Pure closers frequently run out of ground.
main_turf.post_bias: Inside posts hold a massive advantage here. Outside draws (8+) in two-turn turf routes (like 1 1/16 miles) are severely punished due to the incredibly short run into the first turn.

handicapping_angles.turf_tactical_speed: On the grass, aggressively upgrade stalkers and pressers ('P' types) drawn inside. The tight 7-furlong configuration makes it incredibly difficult for deep closers to loop the field.
handicapping_angles.the_mid_atlantic_shipper: Delaware Park is a massive intersection for the Mid-Atlantic circuit. Form lines from Laurel Park, Parx, and Monmouth intersect here. Respect horses dropping out of tough Monmouth or Laurel allowance races into Delaware claimers.
handicapping_angles.the_golden_rail_watch: Always monitor the first few dirt races of the card. If wire-to-wire rail runners dominate, aggressively upgrade inside speed horses for the rest of the day, as DEL can quickly become a 'Golden Rail' speed highway.
